evaluation.manager_summary: Roger opened with no acknowledgement of Tiffany's situation and moved immediately to intake questions. The diagnostic claim at turn 9 — 'it sounds like you have a dependency problem' — is a compliance violation. Clinical screen was minimal, urgency was never assessed, and the next step was vague with a two-day window and no ownership. This call requires urgent coaching review.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent opened with 'Sunrise Recovery Center, this is Roger' and immediately asked 'How long has this been going on?' — no acknowledgement of the caller's courage in reaching out, no warmth, no use of her name. Flat transactional tone throughout the call. Caller volunteered that she thought she had a problem (a moment requiring emotional acknowledgement) and received a data-collection question.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Open with acknowledgement before moving to intake questions. The caller said 'I think I have a problem' — that takes courage and deserves a response before 'how long has this been going on.'

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent captured carrier (Summit Select), member ID (BP66962391), and group number (GRP-155058). Fields were collected in correct order. However the questions were extremely terse ('Member ID?' 'Group number?') with no framing as a step toward care, and no mention of verification timeline or next steps around insurance.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Insurance fields captured correctly but the process felt interrogative. Frame verification as a step toward help, not gatekeeping.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent asked about quantity (how much per day, morning drinking) but missed severity indicators: no withdrawal symptoms question, no duration beyond 'how long has this been going on', no co-occurring concerns. Prior treatment was asked at turn 17 as an afterthought after insurance collection. No context given about why these questions matter.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Clinical screen needs to cover withdrawal risk, severity, and prior treatment history — and these should come before insurance, not after.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: No urgency assessment at any point. Agent did not ask about timing, withdrawal symptoms, or whether the caller was in a safe situation. The vague 'today or tomorrow' callback window is inconsistent with any urgency protocol. A call this short with no urgency check is a gap.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Even for a cooperative caller, ask about urgency and timing — it shapes the next step and ensures you don't miss a caller who needs same-day support.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: N/A — caller is the patient (Tiffany), not a family member.
N/A — no objections were raised by the caller in this call.

Next step was 'someone will call you back about availability.' No name, no ownership, no specific timing. When caller asked 'when?' the answer was 'probably today or tomorrow' — a two-day window with no commitment. Caller asked what to do in the meantime and received 'just wait for our call.' No confirmation email, no intake form, nothing actionable.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Replace 'probably today or tomorrow' with a specific commitment. Name who calls, when, and what they'll cover. Give the caller something to do — even just an email.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: COMPLIANCE VIOLATION at turn 9: 'Based on what you're telling me, it sounds like you have a dependency problem that's going to need professional treatment.' This is a diagnostic claim — intake staff cannot diagnose dependency. The correct language is referral-based: 'our clinical team will assess what level of care is right for you.'
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Never use diagnostic language. You cannot tell a caller they 'have a dependency problem.' Refer all clinical assessment to the clinical team.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Okay. We'll verify that. Any prior treatment?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: How much are you drinking a day?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: And are you drinking in the morning?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Okay. We'll verify that. Any prior treatment?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Okay. Someone will call you back about availability.

compliance_flags.matched_phrase: it sounds like you have a dependency problem
